/docs/docs/filesystem.description: Read, write, mount, and manage files inside agentOS, all backed by a virtual filesystem isolated from the host disk.

/docs/docs.description: Run coding agents inside isolated VMs with full filesystem, process, and network control.

/docs/docs/js-runtime.description: How agentOS runs guest JavaScript: native V8 acceleration, low memory overhead, and Node.js compatibility.

/docs/docs/nodejs-runtime.description: Run Node.js in agentOS: native V8 acceleration, the node CLI, installing packages, and Node.js compatibility.

/docs/docs/permissions.title: Permissions
/docs/docs/permissions.description: The per-scope kernel permission policy that gates every guest syscall in the sandbox.

/docs/docs/persistence.description: How agentOS persists files and manages sleep/wake cycles.

/docs/docs/processes.description: Execute commands, spawn long-running processes, and open interactive shells in agentOS VMs.

/docs/docs/python-runtime.title: Python Runtime
/docs/docs/python-runtime.description: Run Python in agentOS: the python CLI, installing packages ahead of time or at runtime, and what's supported.

/docs/docs/quickstart.description: Set up an agentOS actor, create a session, and run your first coding agent.

/docs/docs/resource-limits.title: Resource Limits
/docs/docs/resource-limits.description: Cap per-VM resources, JavaScript CPU/wall-clock time, Python execution, and WASM runtime work so guest code can never exhaust the host.

/docs/docs/sandbox.description: Extend agentOS with full sandboxes for heavy workloads like browsers, desktop automation, and compilation.

/docs/docs/security-model.description: Trust boundaries, isolation guarantees, and the agentOS threat model.

/docs/docs/sessions.description: Open durable ACP sessions, prompt them, read history, and restore adapters.

/docs/docs/software.title: Software
/docs/docs/software.description: Install software packages and configure the commands available inside agentOS.

/docs/docs/system-prompt.title: System Prompt
/docs/docs/system-prompt.description: How agentOS injects context into agent sessions.

/docs/docs/versus-sandbox.title: agentOS vs Sandbox
/docs/docs/versus-sandbox.description: When to use the lightweight agentOS VM, a full sandbox, or both together.

/docs/docs/webhooks.title: Webhooks
/docs/docs/webhooks.description: Trigger agent sessions directly from external webhooks using Hono.

/docs/docs/workflows.title: Workflow Automation
/docs/docs/workflows.description: Orchestrate multi-step agent tasks with durable workflows.

/docs/docs/agents/claude.title: Claude Code
/docs/docs/agents/claude.description: Run the Claude Code agent inside a VM with skills, MCP servers, and custom configuration.

/docs/docs/agents/codex.title: Codex
/docs/docs/agents/codex.description: Run the Codex coding agent inside a VM with skills, MCP servers, and custom configuration.

/docs/docs/agents/custom.title: Custom Agents
/docs/docs/agents/custom.description: Bring your own coding agent to agentOS by speaking the Agent Client Protocol (ACP) inside the VM.

/docs/docs/agents/opencode.title: OpenCode
/docs/docs/agents/opencode.description: Run the OpenCode coding agent inside a VM with skills, MCP servers, and custom configuration.

/docs/docs/agents/pi.title: Pi
/docs/docs/agents/pi.description: Run the Pi coding agent inside a VM with extensions and custom configuration.

/docs/docs/architecture/agent-sdk-snapsh.title: Agent SDK Snapshots
/docs/docs/architecture/agent-sdk-snapsh.description: How an agent's SDK is evaluated once per sidecar into a V8 heap snapshot and reused across sessions instead of re-imported on every openSession: the bundle, the userland snapshot, the process-wide cache, pre-warm, per-session restore, isolation, and the snapshot-safety rules an SDK must follow.

/docs/docs/architecture/agent-sessions.title: Agent Sessions
/docs/docs/architecture/agent-sessions.description: How durable sessions, ACP adapters, prompts, permissions, and history flow through AgentOS.

/docs/docs/architecture/compiler-toolcha.title: Compiler Toolchain
/docs/docs/architecture/compiler-toolcha.description: How agentOS compiles its command suite to WebAssembly: Rust coreutils via cargo and C programs via wasi-sdk, linked against a patched wasi-libc plus the wasi-ext bindings, and how the resulting .wasm files become the guest's commands.

/docs/docs/architecture/filesystem.title: Filesystem
/docs/docs/architecture/filesystem.description: Internals of the kernel VFS: the overlay/mount/root engines, how guest fs syscalls are routed and confined, WASM preopens, and mount confinement against symlink and .. escapes.

/docs/docs/architecture/javascript-execu.title: JavaScript Executor & Socket Reactor
/docs/docs/architecture/javascript-execu.description: How the process-wide Tokio runtime, bounded socket tasks, durable readiness state, capacity-one VM wakes, and thread-affine V8 executor cooperate without running guest JavaScript on Tokio.

/docs/docs/architecture/limits-and-obser.title: Limits & Observability
/docs/docs/architecture/limits-and-obser.description: How agentOS bounds resources, applies backpressure, warns before a limit is hit, and surfaces it all to the host.

/docs/docs/architecture/networking.title: Networking
/docs/docs/architecture/networking.description: How the kernel socket table works: a single VM-local transport that carries host, JavaScript, and WASM traffic, where fetch / net / dns route through it, how egress policy and loopback confinement are enforced, and how preview URLs are served.

/docs/docs/architecture/packages-and-com.title: Packages & Command Resolution
/docs/docs/architecture/packages-and-com.description: How software is packaged, linked, resolved, and executed in an agentOS VM: a package is a directory, resolution is a $PATH walk, and a file's header picks its runtime.

/docs/docs/architecture/posix-syscalls.title: POSIX Syscalls
/docs/docs/architecture/posix-syscalls.description: How agentOS extends WASI in two layers so WebAssembly guests behave like normal POSIX programs on top of the kernel.

/docs/docs/architecture/processes.title: Processes
/docs/docs/architecture/processes.description: Internals of the kernel process model: the virtual process table, how spawns are serviced, stdio bridging, PTYs, and how WASM sh and coreutils map onto it.

/docs/docs/architecture/sessions-persist.title: Sessions & Persistence
/docs/docs/architecture/sessions-persist.description: How AgentOS stores exact ACP history and restores adapters through VM SQLite.

/docs/docs/architecture/tls-ssl.title: TLS & SSL
/docs/docs/architecture/tls-ssl.description: How agentOS uses in-guest mbedTLS plus a VM CA bundle for curl / wget / git, and a hermetic OpenSSL libcrypto build for OpenSSH.

/docs/docs/custom-software/building-wasm.title: Building Binaries
/docs/docs/custom-software/building-wasm.description: Compile WASM command binaries for agentOS from source.

/docs/docs/custom-software/definition.title: Software Definition
/docs/docs/custom-software/definition.description: The software-package definition for custom commands and agents in an agentOS VM: a package is a packed .aospkg (or a package directory), declared with defineSoftware({ packagePath }).

/docs/docs/custom-software/publishing.title: Publishing Packages
/docs/docs/custom-software/publishing.description: Build, publish, and consume agentOS packages — locally, from npm, or from your own repo.

/docs/docs/frameworks/flue.title: Flue
/docs/docs/frameworks/flue.description: Use agentOS as the durable sandbox backend for Flue.

/docs/docs/frameworks/vercel-eve.title: Vercel Eve
/docs/docs/frameworks/vercel-eve.description: Run Vercel Eve with agentOS and Rivet World.
